questions: What is the correct step ordering and dependency chain for the implementation?
Which files need changes and what is the minimal change for each?
What verification checks can be performed without a live GitHub Release?
Are there any blocking dependencies or risks in the implementation sequence?

answers.answer: The implementation follows an 8-step sequence: (1) Create build-release.yml CI workflow, (2) Delete auto-tag.yml, (3) Rewrite check.ts (GitHub API + auth), (4) Rewrite validate.ts (staged validation), (5) Rewrite perform.ts (staged download+swap, depends on steps 3-4), (6) Update index.ts (orchestration, depends on steps 3-5), (7) Update documentation files (independent of code logic), (8) Quality gates. Steps 1-2 are independent. Steps 3-4 are independent of each other but must precede step 5. Step 6 depends on steps 3-5. Step 7 is independent. Step 8 depends on all prior steps.
evidence: src/update/perform.ts imports from check.ts (GIT_INSTALL_SPEC) and will import from validate.ts
src/update/index.ts imports from check.ts, perform.ts, and validate.ts
Documentation files are independent of update module internals

answers.answer: 13 files are affected: 1 new (.github/workflows/build-release.yml), 1 deleted (.github/workflows/auto-tag.yml), 3 rewritten (src/update/check.ts, perform.ts, validate.ts), 1 updated (src/update/index.ts), 4 documentation updates (src/docs/cli-content.ts, src/skill/show.ts, src/skill/paths.ts, skill-content/references/commands.md). 3 files confirmed unchanged: publish.yml, version.ts, config.ts. The existing InstallSource type already has mode: 'github' with all needed fields — no type changes required.
evidence: src/lib/config.ts:5-11 — InstallSource type already includes mode: 'github', repo, branch, commit, version
src/update/version.ts uses import.meta.url and config SHA — already works for new mechanism
tech-research/tech-research.md confirms no changes needed for version.ts, config.ts, publish.yml

answers.answer: All quality gates (typecheck, build, tests) can run locally. CLI --version can be tested after build via 'node dist/index.js --version'. Static verification of workflow YAML structure, absence of npm install references, and presence of new API patterns can all be done. The only check that cannot run is an end-to-end update test requiring a live GitHub Release, since the CI workflow has not run yet. This is noted in pre-conditions.
evidence: package.json scripts: build (tsc), typecheck (tsc --noEmit), test (tsc && node --test dist/**/*.test.js)
No GitHub Release exists for the 'latest' tag — the workflow is new and has not run

answers.answer: No blocking dependencies exist for the implementation. All changes are within the helix-cli repo. The CI workflow can be created and validated structurally but cannot be tested until it runs on GitHub after merge. The update module rewrite uses only Node.js built-in APIs (fetch, fs, child_process) and requires no new dependencies. The zero-production-dependency constraint is preserved. The main risk is the rename-based swap on Windows, mitigated by retry logic and EXDEV fallback as specified in tech-research.
evidence: tech-research/tech-research.md — cross-platform considerations section confirms tar availability and rename behavior

statement: The implementation plan defines 8 atomic, sequenced steps to replace the hlx self-update mechanism with GitHub Release assets. Steps 1-2 handle CI workflow changes (new build-release.yml, delete auto-tag.yml). Steps 3-6 rewrite the src/update/ module in dependency order: check.ts (GitHub API), validate.ts (staged validation), perform.ts (staged download+swap), index.ts (orchestration). Step 7 updates 4 documentation files to remove npm install references. Step 8 runs quality gates. The verification plan includes 12 required checks covering quality gates, workflow structure, code patterns, and behavioral verification via the built CLI.
